vocabulary.example_cn: 现实是，蒂姆·斯科特明白，他们在上一个选举周期中竞选的每一个议题，共和党和唐纳德·特朗普都表现不佳。

vocabulary.en: in a state of inactivity or dormancy
vocabulary.excerpt: On the one hand, they clearly need to keep Trump relatively quiescent so that he will do what they need to help turn out their voters.
vocabulary.example_cn: 一方面，他们显然需要让特朗普相对安静，这样他才会做他们需要的事情来帮助动员他们的选民。

vocabulary.en: a flash of lightning and a crash of thunder together; a sudden and unexpected event that causes shock or damage
vocabulary.excerpt: If you show independence from Trump, you risk him, you know, throwing a thunderbolt at you and depressing base turnout.
vocabulary.example_cn: 如果你表现出对特朗普的独立性，你就有可能被他突然打击，从而压低基本盘投票率。

vocabulary.en: a public statement of support for a person, product, or idea
vocabulary.excerpt: My group chat is the co-endorsement from the president in our Republican gubernatorial primary.
vocabulary.example_cn: 我的群聊是关于总统在我们共和党州长初选中的共同背书。

sentences.en: The three progressives who were backed by New York City's Democratic socialist mayor, all of them won their House primaries last night.
sentences.cn: 三位得到纽约市民主社会主义市长支持的进步派人士，昨晚都赢得了众议院初选。
sentences.structure: 主语（The three progressives）+ 定语从句（who were backed by...）+ 主句（all of them won...）
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，主句是“all of them won their House primaries last night”，主语“The three progressives”后跟一个由“who”引导的定语从句，修饰“progressives”。

sentences.en: So, you had former city comptroller Brad Lander ousted two-term congressman Dan Goldman after a bruising campaign that focused heavily on their differences over Israel.
sentences.cn: 所以，前市审计长布拉德·兰德在经历了一场激烈且主要围绕以色列问题分歧的竞选后，击败了两任国会议员丹·戈德曼。
sentences.structure: 主语（you）+ 谓语（had）+ 宾语（former city comptroller Brad Lander）+ 宾语补足语（ousted...）+ 时间状语（after...）+ 定语从句（that focused...）
sentences.analysis: 这是一个简单句，但包含复杂的宾语补足语结构。“had”在这里是使役动词，表示“使得”。“ousted”是过去分词作宾语补足语。

sentences.en: But what I'll tell you, what I will tell you is that the big takeaway from the New York primaries last night was New York 17, a moderate candidate who will take on Mike Lawler, a swing district in New York that will help us get the majority.
sentences.cn: 但我要告诉你的是，昨晚纽约初选的最大收获是纽约第17选区，一位温和派候选人将挑战迈克·劳勒，这是一个摇摆选区，将帮助我们获得多数席位。
sentences.structure: 主语从句（what I'll tell you）+ 系动词（is）+ 表语从句（that the big takeaway...）
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，主语由“what”引导的主语从句充当，表语由“that”引导的表语从句充当。表语从句中又包含定语从句修饰“candidate”和“district”。

sentences.en: And if they do get to Washington, they are going to affect the internal dynamics of the caucus.
sentences.cn: 如果他们真的进入华盛顿，他们将影响党团内部的动态。
sentences.structure: 条件状语从句（if they do get to Washington）+ 主句（they are going to affect...）
sentences.analysis: 这是一个条件句，从句使用“do”表示强调，主句使用“be going to”表示将来。

sentences.en: But ultimately, I think whether the left comes out of November with as much momentum as they look like this morning, I think it's going to depend a lot on how those candidates in swing districts do.
sentences.cn: 但最终，我认为左翼在11月之后是否还能保持像今天早上这样的势头，很大程度上取决于那些摇摆选区的候选人的表现。
sentences.structure: 主句（I think）+ 宾语从句（whether...）+ 插入语（I think）+ 主句（it's going to depend...）
sentences.analysis: 这是一个复合句，第一个“I think”后跟一个由“whether”引导的宾语从句，第二个“I think”后跟一个主句，其中包含“how”引导的宾语从句。

sentences.en: And as long as there isn't, you can't impose a national voting regime on red states and blue states.
sentences.cn: 只要没有，你就不能将全国性的投票制度强加给红州和蓝州。
sentences.structure: 条件状语从句（as long as there isn't）+ 主句（you can't impose...）
sentences.analysis: 这是一个条件句，“as long as”引导条件状语从句，主句使用“can't”表示否定。

sentences.en: It is very hard for a Senate candidate, no matter how strong or popular they are personally, to win on ground that usually votes the other way for president.
sentences.cn: 对于一位参议员候选人来说，无论他们个人多么强大或受欢迎，要在通常投票支持另一方总统的选区获胜是非常困难的。
sentences.structure: 形式主语（It）+ 系动词（is）+ 表语（very hard）+ 真正主语（for a Senate candidate...to win...）+ 让步状语（no matter how...）+ 定语从句（that usually votes...）
sentences.analysis: 这是一个使用形式主语的句子，真正的主语是“for a Senate candidate to win...”，其中包含一个让步状语从句和一个定语从句。

sentences.en: Sources tell CNN he fired six political appointees hired under former DNI Tulsi Gabbard.
sentences.cn: 消息人士告诉CNN，他解雇了前国家情报总监图尔西·加巴德任命的六名政治任命官员。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾+宾语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句为'Sources tell CNN'，后接省略that的宾语从句'he fired...'，从句中'fired'为谓语，'six political appointees'为宾语，过去分词短语'hired under former DNI Tulsi Gabbard'作后置定语修饰'appointees'。

sentences.en: The fifth round of peace talks between Israel and Lebanon, mediated by the U.S., took place at the State Department Tuesday.
sentences.cn: 由美国斡旋的以色列和黎巴嫩第五轮和平谈判周二在国务院举行。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾+地点状语+时间状语
sentences.analysis: 主语为'The fifth round of peace talks between Israel and Lebanon'，过去分词短语'mediated by the U.S.'作插入语修饰主语，谓语为'took place'，地点状语'at the State Department'，时间状语'Tuesday'。

sentences.en: Super PACs, partially funded by Claude maker Anthropic, spent a whopping $18 million in support of Alex Bores.
sentences.cn: 由Claude制造商Anthropic部分资助的超级政治行动委员会，花费了高达1800万美元支持亚历克斯·博雷斯。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾+状语
sentences.analysis: 主语为'Super PACs'，过去分词短语'partially funded by Claude maker Anthropic'作后置定语，谓语'spent'，宾语'a whopping $18 million'，介词短语'in support of Alex Bores'作目的状语。

sentences.en: And then a super PAC, partially funded by OpenAI's co-founder, spent $8 million opposing him.
sentences.cn: 然后一个由OpenAI联合创始人部分资助的超级政治行动委员会花费了800万美元反对他。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾+现在分词短语作状语
sentences.analysis: 主语为'a super PAC'，过去分词短语'partially funded by OpenAI's co-founder'作后置定语，谓语'spent'，宾语'$8 million'，现在分词短语'opposing him'作伴随状语。

sentences.en: Both of those bills were opposed by the entire A.I. industry, including by Anthropic.
sentences.cn: 这两项法案都遭到了整个人工智能行业的反对，包括Anthropic。
sentences.structure: 被动语态
sentences.analysis: 主语'Both of those bills'，谓语'were opposed'，介词短语'by the entire A.I. industry'引出动作执行者，现在分词短语'including by Anthropic'进一步补充说明。

sentences.en: It's just that the battle lines in this race, in particular, are whether we can regulate A.I. at all.
sentences.cn: 只是这场竞选中的战线，特别是，在于我们是否能够监管人工智能。
sentences.structure: 主系表+表语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句为'It's just that...'，其中'that'引导表语从句，从句主语为'the battle lines'，系动词'are'，表语为'whether we can regulate A.I. at all'。

sentences.en: And out of the 29 races that they have gotten involved in, they've won 28.
sentences.cn: 在他们参与的29场竞选中，他们赢了28场。
sentences.structure: 介词短语+主谓宾
sentences.analysis: 介词短语'out of the 29 races'作状语，后接定语从句'that they have gotten involved in'修饰'races'，主句为'they've won 28'。

sentences.en: What's really interesting about Palantir talking point in all of this saga is that the co-founder of Leading the Future is a Palantir co-founder as well.
sentences.cn: 关于Palantir在整个事件中的话题点，真正有趣的是Leading the Future的联合创始人也是Palantir的联合创始人。
sentences.structure: 主语从句+系动词+表语从句
sentences.analysis: 主语从句'What's really interesting about Palantir talking point in all of this saga'作主语，系动词'is'，表语从句'that the co-founder of Leading the Future is a Palantir co-founder as well'。

sentences.en: And he went after Bores over Bores' ties to Palantir, even though Bores left because of ethical concerns.
sentences.cn: 他因为博雷斯与Palantir的关系而攻击他，尽管博雷斯是因为道德问题离开的。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾+状语从句
sentences.analysis: 主句为'he went after Bores'，介词短语'over Bores' ties to Palantir'作原因状语，后接让步状语从句'even though Bores left because of ethical concerns'。

sentences.en: If you are going to disrupt the economic and therefore political power significantly of one party's base, highly educated, often female voters who vote mostly Democrat and military and working class people who do not feel supported, and you feel like that's -- you believe that that's going to work out politically, you're in an insane asylum.
sentences.cn: 如果你要严重破坏一个政党基本盘的经济和政治权力，这些基本盘包括受过高等教育、通常投票给民主党的女性选民，以及感到不被支持的军人和工人阶级，而你觉得——你相信这在政治上行得通，那你就是在疯人院里。
sentences.structure: 条件状语从句+主句
sentences.analysis: 条件状语从句由'If'引导，从句中'you are going to disrupt'为谓语，宾语为'the economic and therefore political power significantly of one party's base'，后接两个并列的定语从句修饰'base'：'highly educated, often female voters who vote mostly Democrat'和'military and working class people who do not feel supported'。主句为'you're in an insane asylum'。

sentences.en: But what -- that was driven mostly on cultural grounds.
sentences.cn: 但这主要是由文化因素驱动的。
sentences.structure: 被动语态
sentences.analysis: 主语'that'指代前文内容，谓语'was driven'，介词短语'on cultural grounds'作方式状语。

sentences.en: What's happening in the A.I. era is you get a reinforcement on economic grounds, where you now have, I think, in white collar America, a lot of the economic anxiety that really helped drive blue collar America in the other way in the '80s.
sentences.cn: 在人工智能时代发生的是，你在经济层面上得到了强化，我认为，现在在美国白领阶层中，有很多经济焦虑，这种焦虑在80年代曾帮助推动蓝领阶层走向另一个方向。
sentences.structure: 主语从句+系动词+表语从句
sentences.analysis: 主语从句'What's happening in the A.I. era'作主语，系动词'is'，表语从句'you get a reinforcement on economic grounds'，后接非限制性定语从句'where you now have...'修饰'economic grounds'，定语从句中插入语'I think'，宾语'a lot of the economic anxiety'后接定语从句'that really helped drive blue collar America in the other way in the '80s'。

sentences.en: And it tends -- the economics is going to push in the same direction as the culture.
sentences.cn: 而且它倾向于——经济将朝着与文化相同的方向推进。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾+比较结构
sentences.analysis: 主语'the economics'，谓语'is going to push'，介词短语'in the same direction'，后接比较结构'as the culture'。

sentences.en: And it's something that's important to me.
sentences.cn: 这对我来说很重要。
sentences.structure: 主系表+定语从句
sentences.analysis: 主语'It'，系动词'is'，表语'something'，后接定语从句'that's important to me'。

sentences.en: It's just not easy.
sentences.cn: 这并不容易。
sentences.structure: 主系表
sentences.analysis: 主语'It'，系动词'is'，表语'not easy'。

topics.title: 2026年纽约州初选与民主党内部左转
topics.content: 2026年6月23日，纽约州举行国会众议院初选。纽约市民主社会主义市长佐赫兰·马姆达尼支持的候选人取得全面胜利，包括布拉德·兰德击败现任议员丹·戈德曼，克莱尔·瓦尔德兹和达里亚利扎·阿维拉·切瓦利尔也分别获胜。这标志着民主党内部进步派势力的崛起，尤其是在以色列政策等议题上。分析人士指出，虽然这些胜利主要发生在安全选区，但可能影响民主党未来的内部动态和全国选举策略。
topics.keywords: 纽约初选, 民主党, 进步派, 马姆达尼, 以色列

topics.title: 特朗普与参议院共和党人的冲突：SAVE法案与阻挠议事
topics.content: 特朗普总统推动的《SAVE法案》（选民身份验证法案）在参议院遭遇阻力。参议院共和党领袖约翰·图恩表示，废除阻挠议事规则以通过该法案不现实，因为缺乏足够支持。同时，参议院通过了一项战争权力决议，要求总统在继续对伊朗战争前获得国会批准。这反映了特朗普与部分共和党参议员之间的紧张关系，以及参议院内部关于权力平衡的争论。
topics.keywords: 特朗普, 参议院, SAVE法案, 阻挠议事, 战争权力决议

topics.title: AI与网络安全威胁
topics.content: 节目中提及一个严峻的情报警告：下一次重大网络攻击可能就在几个月后，且人类可能与之无关。这暗示了人工智能驱动的网络攻击的潜在风险。随着AI技术的发展，网络安全威胁日益复杂，可能对国家安全和关键基础设施构成挑战。
topics.keywords: 网络安全, AI, 网络攻击, 情报警告

topics.title: 肯尼迪家族的政治遗产
topics.content: 约翰·F·肯尼迪的唯一孙子在纽约初选中失利，引发了对肯尼迪家族政治遗产（常被称为“卡米洛特”）的讨论。这反映了政治世家的影响力在当代美国政治中的变化，以及选民对传统政治家族的接受度。
topics.keywords: 肯尼迪, 卡米洛特, 政治遗产, 初选

topics.title: NCAA运动员资格规则改革
topics.content: NCAA批准了对一级运动员资格规则的全面改革，允许运动员在大学生涯中参加五个赛季的比赛，取消了红衫和豁免制度。这一改革将影响大学体育的竞争格局和运动员的职业生涯规划。
topics.keywords: NCAA, 运动员资格, 改革, 大学体育

topics.title: New York Congressional Primary and AI Regulation
topics.content: The Democratic primary in New York's 12th congressional district saw significant spending from AI-related super PACs. Micah Lasher won, while Alex Bores, a pro-regulation candidate, was backed by Anthropic-funded PACs and opposed by OpenAI-funded PACs. The race highlights the growing influence of AI companies in politics and the debate over AI regulation.
topics.keywords: New York primary, AI regulation, super PAC, Anthropic, OpenAI, Micah Lasher, Alex Bores

topics.title: Kennedy Legacy in Politics
topics.content: Jack Schlossberg, JFK's only grandson, lost the Democratic primary in New York, finishing third. The discussion explores whether the Kennedy name still holds political power, with panelists noting that name recognition alone is not enough in modern elections, especially among younger voters.
topics.keywords: Kennedy, Jack Schlossberg, political dynasty, name recognition, primary loss

topics.title: Housing Affordability Bill
topics.content: Congress passed a bipartisan housing affordability bill that limits private equity from buying homes, eases zoning and permitting rules, and promotes manufactured housing. The bill reflects growing public concern over housing costs and is seen as a rare bipartisan achievement.
topics.keywords: housing affordability, bipartisan, private equity, zoning, manufactured homes, Congress

topics.title: AI and Job Displacement
topics.content: The transcript discusses the economic anxiety caused by AI, particularly among white-collar workers, and compares it to the blue-collar job losses of the 1980s. CEOs are making alarmist statements about job displacement while rapidly deploying AI, raising questions about the true intent.
topics.keywords: AI, job displacement, white-collar, blue-collar, economic anxiety, CEOs

topics.title: Trump Administration and Intelligence Community
topics.content: Acting DNI Bill Pulte fired political appointees and removed career officials, signaling a push to shrink the intelligence agency. This reflects ongoing tensions between the Trump administration and the intelligence community.
topics.keywords: DNI, Bill Pulte, intelligence community, political appointees, agency cuts

topics.title: Israel-Lebanon Peace Talks and Iran
topics.content: The fifth round of U.S.-mediated peace talks between Israel and Lebanon took place, with Iran threatening to leave negotiations over Israeli strikes. Secretary of State Marco Rubio emphasized that the talks are separate from U.S.-Iran negotiations, though Iran's support for Hezbollah will be discussed.
topics.keywords: Israel, Lebanon, peace talks, Iran, Hezbollah, Marco Rubio

topics.title: NATO and Trump
topics.content: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte, known as the 'Trump whisperer,' met with President Trump ahead of the NATO summit. Trump continues to threaten leaving NATO, highlighting ongoing tensions between the U.S. and the alliance.
topics.keywords: NATO, Mark Rutte, Trump, summit, alliance
